heading: Construction and charter of vessels for unsuccessful routes
label: 57531 Construction and charter of vessels for unsuccessful routes
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)5753146 App.:1204., title VII, § 714, ; , ; , ; , , ; , , ; , , ; , , .June 29, 1936, ch. 85849 Stat. 2011June 23, 1938, ch. 600, § 3252 Stat. 962Aug. 4, 1939, ch. 417, § 1253 Stat. 1186Pub. L. 97–31, § 12(116)95 Stat. 164Mar. 18, 1959June 12, 1960Oct. 21, 1970Aug. 6, 1981In subsection (a)(2), the words “demise charter” are substituted for “demise . . . or bare-boat charter” to eliminate unnecessary words. The words “operator of vessels of the United States” are substituted for “American-flag operator” for consistency in the revised title.Subsection (b)(2) is substituted for “Upon the basis of a twenty-five year life of the vessel” because of (, ), which provided that “Nothing in any amendment made by this Act [including section 5 substituting ‘twenty-five’ for ‘twenty’ in 46 App. U.S.C. 1204] shall operate or be interpreted to change from twenty to twenty-five years the provisions of the Merchant Marine Act, 1936, as amended, relating to the commercial expectancy or period of depreciation of any tanker or other liquid bulk carrier.”section 9 of Public Law 86–51874 Stat. 217June 12, 1960In subsection (c), in paragraphs (1) and (4), references to the useful life specified in subsection (b)(2) are substituted for references to the twenty-five year useful life for the reason stated in explaining subsection (b)(2).

children.text: have constructed, in private shipyards or in navy yards, vessels of the types necessary for the trade route; and
demise charter those new vessels to the operator of vessels of the United States established on the trade route.

children.label: A
children.text: a percentage of the depreciated foreign cost computed annually determined by the Secretary of the Treasury, taking into consideration the current average market yield on outstanding marketable obligations of the Government with remaining periods to maturity comparable to the term of the charter, adjusted to the nearest one-eighth percent; and

children.label: B
children.text: an allowance adequate in the judgment of the Secretary of Transportation to cover administrative costs.

children.label: A
children.text: a 25-year life for dry-cargo and passenger vessels; and

children.label: B
children.text: a 20-year life for tankers and other bulk liquid carrier vessels.
the purchase price shall be the foreign cost less depreciation to the date of purchase based on the useful life specified in subsection (b)(2);
the required cash payment payable at the time of the purchase shall be 25 percent of the purchase price;

the charter may provide that any part of the charter hire paid in excess of the minimum charter hire provided for in this section may be credited against the cash payment payable at the time of the purchase;

the balance of the purchase price shall be paid within the remaining years of useful life (as specified in subsection (b)(2)) after the date of delivery of the vessel under the charter and in approximately equal annual installments, except that the first installment, which shall be payable on the next ensuing anniversary date of the delivery under the charter, shall be a proportionate part of the annual installment; and

children.label: A
children.text: a rate determined by the Secretary of the Treasury, taking into consideration the current average market yield on outstanding marketable obligations of the Government with remaining periods to maturity comparable to the average maturities of the loans, adjusted to the nearest one-eighth percent; plus

children.label: B
children.text: an allowance adequate in the judgment of the Secretary of Transportation to cover administrative costs.

children.label: C
children.text: on a round voyage from the west coast of the United States to a European port that includes an intercoastal port of the United States;

children.label: D
children.text: on a round voyage from the Atlantic coast of the United States to the Orient that includes an intercoastal port of the United States; or

children.label: E
children.text: on a voyage in foreign trade on which the vessel may stop at Hawaii or an island territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: .—Domestic trade
children.label: 2 .—Domestic trade
children.text: The charter shall provide if the vessel is operated in domestic trade on any of the services specified in paragraph (1), the charterer will pay annually to the Secretary of Transportation that proportion of ⁄ of the difference between the domestic and foreign cost of the vessel as the gross revenue derived from the domestic trade bears to the gross revenue derived from the entire voyages completed during the preceding year.125
